stem: A 116-meter zip line cable runs from the top of an observation tower to a landing platform on the ground. The cable makes a 44-degree angle with the ground. Given that sin 44° ≈ 20/29, cos 44° ≈ 21/29, and tan 44° ≈ 20/21, what is the horizontal distance from the base of the tower to the landing platform, in meters?
explanation: In this right triangle, the zip line cable is the hypotenuse (116 meters) and the horizontal distance is the side adjacent to the 44° angle. Using cos 44° = adjacent/hypotenuse: adjacent = 116 × cos 44° = 116 × (21/29) = (116 ÷ 29) × 21 = 4 × 21 = 84 meters.

distractorRationale.A: Used wrong special triangle ratio — applied the 30-60-90 rule (short leg = hypotenuse/2) instead of using the given trig value: 116/2 = 58
distractorRationale.B: Used wrong special triangle ratio — applied the 45-45-90 rule (leg = hypotenuse/√2) instead of using the given trig value: 116/√2 = 58√2
distractorRationale.C: Used sin instead of cos — found the vertical height (opposite) instead of the horizontal distance (adjacent): 116 × (20/29) = 80
